Exploration of the ongoing debate surrounding the integration of Artificial Intelligence in the art world. This collection depicts the Anti-AI movement visualized as old men (the old way of thinking) locked in battle with the pro-AI art movement represented as The Vandals. creator.bio: 0xdgb is aFull-time digital artist who approaches his work through the frame of minimalism. With over a decade of experience in illustration and graphic design, his works capture the subtle beauty of what might otherwise be seen as the mundane and often revolve around art and storytelling. \n\nHis most popular works depict native Web3 and NFT events — refined to their most relatable forms. For the artist, his works are not only a method of self-expression but also a means of self-soothing. Simply put, he makes art that he enjoys, in hopes that others might as well. Examples of this are found in his signature surveillance camera. A symbol which might be viewed as a personification of the constant surveillance of our digital lives or perhaps the transparency provided by blockchain technology. Whatever it may be, 0xdgb leaves interpretation up to the viewer and lets his art speak for him.

metadata.name: JunyAxD_2.png
metadata.description:
My favorite thing about this artwork is the dimensionality and the immediacy achieved.  It is quite special to me, not only because I "evolved" the original drawing, but because the character Dots almost interjects himself in the artwork. The figure is in a kneeling position, which resembles prayer, and is in an environment intended to be an allusion to the metaverse. Dots and Aleqth Unify in this piece, and although the ratio is uncertain, it is evident by the primary figures left (right) ear. 

I came up with the name "Junya" for the original drawing in listening to Kanye West's "Junya". It goes something like "Junya Wantanabe on my WRIST!" with a church organ and infectious baseline in the background. The sonics of the tune inspired a  sort of  intense reverence, represented in this artwork by the gnashing teeth and the impression of immense gravity.  I also see this piece as a draft-work for a much larger sculpture.

metadata.name: Always Watching
metadata.description:
Vagabond 

| Noun: a person who wanders from place to place without a home or job. |

In November 2023, I moved out of my apartment in Denver, Colorado to spend an undetermined period of time trying to see as much of the world as possible. I spent time driving through Colorado, Montana, Mexico, and other various locations. The pinnacle of this period, however, was spending 3 months exploring various parts of Europe with no real agenda. During my time there, I had no real "home," no determined source of income, and felt like I had no clear direction in my life, which turned into being the freest I have felt in a long time. 

Although it was stressful and a bit frightening, constantly staring into the unknown, it was one of the best periods of my life so far. Temporarily unburdened by the day-to-day stressors of “real” life that I had become used to, I spent this period exploring and photographing scenes from Italy, Spain, Portugal, Austria, France, and more. It was the most present I have ever felt without constantly worrying about my future. 

This series aims to showcase the beauty of European cities and landscapes while exemplifying a sense of true freedom and never-ending adventure. 

Each piece with a winning bid of 0.15 ETH or over will come with a signed physical fine art print.

Location: Innsbruck, Austria  
Camera: Leica M6  
Film: Portra 400

metadata.name: This Year I Devour
metadata.description: This painting is based on the sin of "Gluttony". The third sin depicted in my series. It serves as a metaphor for the power an individual can feel with overindulgence, even if it comes at the expense of others suffering. This sin knows no moderation or self restraint yet, it is not as simple as food. Wanting to devour, feels powerful, even sensual at times, but it is a temporary gratification. Appetite isn’t necessarily bad, like christian theologians might argue. Yet it is an appetite that is misdirected, gluttony can only be triumphed by real emotional fulfillment.

metadata.name: Immortal
metadata.description:
When you become a parent you become immortal. Your values, traits, mannerisms and your DNA are transferred from your body to your child. You live on through your children and hope that they achieve what you didn’t, experience the things you didn’t dare try, and live a better life than you did. 
 
Immortal is a piece that addresses these themes and more. It began as a painting of my son staring down at a model skull. Throughout history the skull has been a symbol of mortality, and in that tradition this painting also questions who we are, why we are here, and what will come tomorrow. The painting was set on fire and destroyed but not until it was tokenized, rendering the NFT the original and making it immortal, living forever on the blockchain.
 
The winner of this auction will receive both the  1:1 NFT of the painting as well as a 1:1 NFT of the video. 

View Immortal Burn video here: vimeo.com/520780246

metadata.name: Cthulhu Awakens
metadata.description:
Number 2; This is my interpretation
of the immensely popular H.P.
Lovecraft creation and the second one in the
series of nine. I think it's safe to say this is the most popular painting I've ever done with over 65 million views and 1,4 million likes on Twitter alone. It's been used as reference for upcoming film and TV productions, been publicized in books and articles and spread across the internet in a way I could have never imagined.

It took almost 2 months (on and off) to finish. By far the longest I’ve ever spent on a painting. The goal was as simple as it was challenging - to achieve the feeling of true cosmic horror in the spirit of H.P. Lovecraft. The existential crisis we might face upon realizing our insignificance, but also the visual reminder of standing strong in the face of insurmountable odds.
Follow the journey at https://andreewallin.com/Cthulhu-Collection

metadata.name: Solitary Faith
metadata.description:
The church of Mušov on this small island is the last reminder of the only village before it disappeared in the floods of the reservoir in 1980.

Today, all you see is an abandoned church on a small island in the middle of a lake with nothing around it.

I imagine how the villagers used to come together here on Sunday to pray. Children running around and playing tag in the forecourt. The priest greeted everyone individually and afterwards they went for a coffee together. 
Memories....soaked in water.

metadata.name: Journey to Self-Actualization: Clouded Sky
metadata.description: Our minds are dreaming us into existence, having us caught up in its fantasies and nightmares through thoughts, all of which revolve around a sense of “I”. Most of the time these thoughts permeate an endless “to do” list or for some, inadequacies, reeling them into its storm and fever dreams. Making one falsely believe that once those issues have been solved peace will come—the storm will stop. However, regardless of how careful one is it’s impossible to avoid some storms—fear, sadness, and anger—these emotions are part of our basic biological programming. Ironically, in our effort to protection ourselves from this agony, we create a life that naturally elicit it. It is only when we are not lost in the dream, focused on the present and not acting out the pattern of our conditioning that true happiness can flourish.

metadata.name: The Red Man
metadata.description: I painted The Red Man at what I like to think was perhaps the end of an artistic era, and the beginning of a new one--one that I had no idea was even emerging. For years, I had held my idols close to my heart, painting step by step after them. The Red Man was the first time I let go, and held on to myself instead. This was a painting of grace. One that it felt the universe helped me make. I had just turned 20, my life a blur (I had no money), but the day I posted this on Twitter, the painting garnering millions of views and my life changed forever. People ask me a lot, "What inspired the painting?" and I smile and respond, "Life." This was a distillation of everything I had known and learnt in these years. All my pain, my tears, my laughs and my joys. It's a painting I'm very proud of.

metadata.name: Solitude Room
metadata.description:
Introverts are prone to being anxious. Extroverts can be anxious, too, but it’s different and a bit more difficult for introverts, since the world isn’t always made for introverts

In a world that demands and thrives on outgoing personalities and constant smiles, even when that’s not realistic, introverts tend to get left behind.

Fitting into a social gathering  may seem exhausting for introverts. Constant anxiety and nervousness when engaging with people, especially those who are out of our comfort zone, feels inhibiting. Moreover, one gets misunderstood on many levels by extroverts, and this adds to our frustrations when trying to communicate.

Just like extroverts, introverts need close relationships to thrive. They simply go about socializing differently and just because something’s different doesn’t mean it’s wrong or inferior. The world needs the introvert’s way, too. 

It is just easier for introverts to go and do something themselves without having to faff about getting others interested or motivated enough to bother.

SOLITUDE ROOM is a representation of all, who struggles as an introvert but keeps walking as if calm despite being dizzy, tired, and full of anxiety.

metadata.name: Good morning.
metadata.description:
Before the sun spills over jagged line,
And paints, in half-speed, the sky with candescent stroke;
I'm met by the hymn of wind, and knot, and rhyme,
As dawn chorus readies in the oak.

On dewy green, I tread muted through dappled rays,
To stir what night's cold breath has left,
And spin to melodies that wake from over way,
While fading dark leaves moon and stars bereft.

It’s here in stillness as vantages withdraw,
And prelude patterns play for all to learn, 
I savour these fruits left at today’s door,
Save only a wish,  for tomorrow's return.

.....

A signed print for the winning bid is included.

metadata.name: Star Cluster within the Carina Nebula
metadata.description:
What an era to be alive!!!

On July 12th, 2022 we witnessed history being made.

A telescope that took decades to build and send to outer space created its 1st images putting another milestone on the unveiling beauties of the heavens and understanding of the early universe.
One of the four images revealed was a very special star-forming region. It is a portion of an emission nebula, NGC 3324, within the famous Carina Nebula. Carina Nebula is one of the brightest and largest complex areas of bright and dark nebulosity in the constellation Carina. It is located in the Carina-Sagittarius Arm of the Milkyway Galaxy and is approximately 8,500 light-years from Earth.
A part of the Carina Nebula, NGC 3324, is an open star cluster closely associated with an emission nebula where new stars are born. The stellar winds from these newly born extremely energetic stars ionize the atoms around and make them glow in different colors. The stellar winds also create the rim-like region in the western section of the nebula where the Hubble Space Telescope is observed in detail, the same section was one of the 1st observations of the James Webb Telescope for comparison.

What is even more mind-blowing is we can observe and take photos of the same regions from ground-based telescopes. The difference between a ground-based telescope and a telescope in space is extremely huge. On the surface of our planet when we do observations and astrophotography, we have to deal with a thick atmosphere and the turbulences in different layers of this atmosphere.

The so-called atmospheric effect is a pain for ground-based telescopes. It requires special hardware and special software to overcome these effects. Thanks to cutting-edge technology, big scientific ground-based telescopes have adaptive optics that can fix the atmospheric effect. By changing the physical geometry of the telescope mirror, we can adapt and ignore the atmospheric effect/distortion on the final image.

For astrophotography and amateur astronomy, adaptive optics hardware and/or software are prohibitively expensive. For this reason, we astrophotographers deal with the atmospheric effect differently by using a process called “Deconvolution”.

Deconvolution is a tedious process of reverse engineering the “blurring” effect of the atmosphere. The imperfections (blurring or in other words convolution) are inferred from a “point spread function” – a mathematical description of the blurring, which can be determined from stars in the image.  “Deconvolution” needs rigorous work and may take well some hours to reduce the atmospheric effect/blurring in a very subtle but effective way.

This image of the NGC 3324 star cluster and emission nebula was shot in Chilean Andes at an elevation of 1525 m from the sea level with a 24” mirrored telescope that doesn't have the adaptive optics system to reduce the atmospheric effect/blurring.

Having achieved imaging the objects on ground based telescopes and equipment that can also be imaged by multi-million, billion dollars equipment is a remarkable achievement. Working to process those images gives you nothing but shivers and goosebumps.


Equipment & Exif
Planewave CDK24 Telescope
Aperture: 610 mm (24 inches)
Focal Length: 3962 mm
F-ratio: 6.5
Mount: Mathis MI-1000/1250 with absolute encoders
Camera: FLI PL 9000
Filters(50mm square): Ha (3nm), OIII (3nm)
Off-Axis guider: Astrodon MonsterMOAG
Guiding camera: Starlight Xpress Ultrastar
Observatory name: El Sauce Observatory
Location: Río Hurtado, Coquimbo Region, Chile
Total 13 Hours Integration
SII 25 x 600 seconds
Ha 26 x 600 seconds
OIII 25 x 600 seconds

1/1 Edition
Artist: Mustafa Aydın
Image Credits: Mustafa Aydın / Telescope Live

metadata.name: Along the Westminster Bridge
metadata.description: “Along the Westminster Bridge” marks a significant moment in my life. A time of return to a country I had only been once before and for similar purposes. The first was to document a moment in musical history—a Skrillex showcase for his label, OWSLA (it was also the first time I had the fortune of traveling internationally for work). The second was to document another moment in Musical history—the premiere of the Back to the Future Musical. This return journey also allowed me to meet with some iconic SuperRare artists in their homes and hometowns. // The complementary colors you see before you (blues and oranges) are a reflection of these two moments in my life, intentionally captured with light leaks through my Leica M4 on Fuji C200 film stock. Both colors exist harmoniously together, just as these two opportunities marked significant moments in my life where I truly felt I had reached a new peak in my personal life and professional career. The bleeding of the two colors symbolizes how one cannot exist without the other. Just as the blue sky meets the orange Earth with spontaneous definition and design, so do these significant moments in our lives. Take each moment for what it’s worth—the value of your ability to live it and the potential for it to one day change your life.

metadata.name: Open World
metadata.description:
As an adventure photographer, there are times I find myself in places that make me question whether or not this is real life...

I was on assignment photographing the Islands of Tahiti with French Polynesia Tourism. We set off to the one-of-a-kind island known as Bora Bora. I thought to myself; how amazing would it be to photograph this magical place from the sky? I convinced the tourism board to send me up for a "doors off" helicopter ride. I reached the heliport, met the pilot, harnessed up, and we flew into the sky. The harness enabled me to extend my whole body out of the door as we soared above the ocean. I told the pilot to fly away from the island and ascend as high as possible. The pilot told me that most flights stay closer to the island but I had a vision and insisted that we flew away.

From this angle, Bora Bora looked like an open world CGI map from a video game. As I was capturing images I was lost in thought and imagining myself dropping out of the chopper with the squad to snag some loot and have a battle royal. I was completely enveloped in one of my childhood fantasies.

I snapped back to reality and realized that my entire body was hanging out of the helicopter, wind was whipping through my hair and I was thousands of feet above the water. I looked at this tiny video game island and focused. I held my breath, ignored the chaos of the extreme conditions I was in, harnessed my adrenaline rush, clicked the shutter on my camera, and captured the most unique photograph I have ever made in my entire career.

What kid didn’t want to be an astronaut when they grew up? I had big dreams of going to space camp, years of training, and one day getting to walk amongst the stars. But that life didn’t pan out for many reasons. After finding photography it didn’t take long to realize that shooting the moon was probably the closest I was ever going to get to achieving my childhood dream, and honestly I couldn’t be happier with where things ended up.

Photography has meant more to me than anything else in this universe. As photographers we have the power to freeze time. creator.bio: Landscape/wildlife/abstract nature photographer with a passion for conservation. My photography focuses on the Rocky Mountains, CA, and Hawaii

metadata.name: Matriarch
metadata.description:
My heart and soul are in this piece. Grizzly bears are my favorite animal and my first choice to photograph on any given day. They have also been the most frustrating aspect of photography as capturing them is never easy. I worked so hard to capture this moment. It took over two years, six trips to Grand Teton National Park, lodging and camping costs, and over 6,500 miles on my car - just to spend about three minutes with famous grizzly sow 399 and her four adult cubs. It was so worth it. About three weeks after I took this photograph, 399 left her cubs to fend for themselves as is customary around two and a half years of age. Photos like this will never be captured again, and that makes this an incredibly special photo that I will forever cherish. 

I have spent so much time thinking about why this moment and this photo became such an obsession for me over the last two years.  First, I have watched and followed this grizzly bear for over a decade now and she became an ambassador for the conservation and protection of endangered species in the Greater Yellowstone Ecosystem years ago. Two years ago, she came out of hibernation at the age of 24 with quadruplets, which is almost unheard of. The fact that in her old age, she was successful in raising four cubs to adulthood is beyond anything people could have ever expected.  Also, very few people have ever witnessed a scene like this. Most grizzlies do not live near roads, but 399 always chose these areas to protect her cubs from male bears that would kill the cubs to mate with her. This gave people in Grand Teton National park a chance to view her over the years. To see these five grizzly bears together was nothing short of magical.  Matriarch 399 showed us these past two years what it looks like to rise to incredible challenges with such love, beauty, and strength. She gave us hope and something to find joy in during a couple of very hard years and proved to all why she is a legend as a wildlife mother. 

I came across this quote a few weeks ago and realized it is why I am so passionate about wildlife and especially predators in the wild. “Wilderness without wildlife is just scenery.” - Lois Crisler. 

There is a second image that I am including with the piece that shows four of the five bears (you can find this in my Twitter thread about this piece). There is one cub that was always wandering and it was difficult to capture all five together. The second shot only has three cubs with mom, but it is another favorite image of mine. I will airdrop this image to the collector once the auction is settled. 

The entire story of this experience is on my website https://www.flatironsphotography.com/2022/05/13/labor-of-love/ for anyone who would like to read about the entire experience and more about the importance of conservation and protecting endangered species.

metadata.name: When the sun and planes align
metadata.description:
In February 2022, I had the immense chance to capture that unique moment.

Using a specially adapted telescope, I observe the solar chromosphere in the narrow band of alpha hydrogen, with a wavelength of 656.28 nm. 

By capturing the solar chromosphere at a precise moment in time, the chromosphere is like fingerprints, visible from all around the universe and offering the possibility of dating my work precisely to the hour in Universal Time (UTC).

Collectors will thus have in their hands a visual archive of the evolution of our Sun, bearing witness to its history and timeless beauty.

Features :

-Altitude of the plane: 12 193 meters 
-Speed of the plane: 883 km/h 
-Distance to the camera: 39 km
-Stacking of two captures taken on 2022/02/26 at 16:57 UTC 

Photography 3748 x 3748 px

metadata.name: A Letter to my Abusive Ex
metadata.description:
Dear ____,

all the things you did to me and said to me, never came true. You know how you said I would be nothing? That my art was never good enough? How you ripped away my freedom and family from? You said no one would ever love me or want to ever be around me? How I would never succeed at anything? How I was a piece of shit? Well, look at me now ____. 

Do you see? Do you see what I have become? I can only thank you for the fact you made me stronger. You made me a warrior within my own skin. You tried to tear me apart. You tried to break me down. My spirit and my soul. You tried to ruin me. You know all the tears I constantly shed for you. How I told to remedy those tears with a smoke. You tried to make me an empty vessel. 

Do you see ____ you were never successful in destroying me. Again, you only made me stronger. I never want you in my life again. You torment my dreams enough but just know... you can never destroy me. I will only become stronger.

Chi

metadata.name: INCONSTANCY
metadata.description:
Oil on Canvas 80x80 cm 
On the surface of swift-running water you cannot make out the reflections of objects near or distant. Even if it is not muddy, even if it is free of foam, reflections in the ceaselessly wavering ripples, the boisterously shifting race are deceptive, vague, incomprehensible.
Only when, from stream to stream, the current has reached a placid estuary, or in still backwaters, or in small lakes with never a tremulous wave, can we see in the mirror-smooth surface the smallest leaf of a tree on the bank, every fiber of a fine-combed cloud, and the intense blue depths of the sky.
So it is with you and me. If, try as we may, we never have been and never shall be able to see, to reflect the truth in all its eternal fresh-minted clarity, is it not because we are still in motion, still living?...

metadata.name: Neo Noir New York: Reveries
metadata.description:
“Don’t you see?” She wondered aloud, eyes opened wide to catch the fading pastels of the summer sunset, which hung like an oil painting over the jagged silhouette of the skyline. One hand rested on her hip, her thumb through a belt loop, and the other extended, aimed up into the distance, like an arrow pulled back on a bowstring. “See what?” I asked, more to hear the sound of her voice than to uncover any deeper meaning. “The clouds,” she explained, “they’re the same as on any grey, dreary day, except for the way the sun is sitting, just beyond the horizon, throwing warm light into the canopy. And where we stand, it’s so specific, that not a single soul will see what we see.” She turned to look at me, her eyes flashed with thought, and I realized it was the deeper meaning I was after. Sometimes, the only way to feel right is to be wrong, and measure what you learn by the distance in between.


-Dave Krugman

metadata.name: Word. 8 - // Nineteen Eighty-Four
metadata.description:
Glad to introduce the 8th algorithm output artwork in the Word. collection on SuperRare. In this piece we are taking a trip to England, London 1949 and dive deep into George Orwell's novel Nineteen Eighty-Four. 

This book has so much influence on the way many of us think because it taps into universal concerns about power, freedom, and the human spirit, issues that have never been so relevant as they are in this century and the coming century ahead of us.

Orwell paints a chilling picture of a society where government controls every aspect of life, from history to language to even thoughts. His portrayal of extreme authoritarianism through the fictional government of "The Party" has remained relevant as a warning against the dangers of unchecked power. The concept of "Big Brother" watching everyone at all times is a masterclass to the idea of pervasive surveillance and loss of privacy and its potential consequences. This theme has only become more relevant with the rise of digital technology, as many people now live in a world where their activities are constantly monitored and as we get used to living with AI agents in all our devices, this concept is taking a whole new dimension nowadays.

--

Collection overview:

Over a period of 33 days, the Word. collection will evolve daily to encompass various historically significant texts, each rendered into unique visual representations through an evolving generative algorithm. Each piece serves as a focal point, capturing moments where written words have irrevocably altered the course of human evolution in various languages and from different cultures and eras.

metadata.name: Dazzle
metadata.description:
This series of abstract portraits explore different facets of the human condition such as joy, suffering, and identity. These works move away from realistic depictions, instead using fragmented and distorted forms to represent the human figure. This abstraction allows the pieces to dynamically represent the interplay of internal and external forces that shape human experiences and perceptions.

The use of varied textures in the artwork adds a tactile depth that enhances the thematic expression of each portrait. These textures range from rough, illustrating psychological turmoil, to smooth, suggesting serenity and introspection. By employing abstract forms and deliberate textural contrasts, the series encourages a deeply personal interpretation, prompting viewers to reflect on the complexities of human emotions and their own place within the broader human experience.

Mixed media: AI + Digital Painting by @dehiscenceart, April 2024.

he’d feed us when he thought we needed it
though we always did
though not always because we were hungry
we’d sit at the grill
and try and recall something
to tell someone
who instead looked at the meat
or rice in the flames of his eyes

and not a story that wasn’t ours
and there wasn’t really any flame left in his eyes anyway
just unseasoned rice
some days I thought I could hear mom
remembering a summer vacation we didn’t have
through the connecting doors
at a neglected motel
other days it was the sound of disintegrating pills
and I wasn’t sure they were all that different

metadata.name: Midnight Glory
metadata.description: North of the arctic circle near the town of Ilulissat in the remote landscapes of Greenland lies an ocean full of icebergs. Tranquil as ever, untouched, and floating around until they melt into water. While I was on a sailing expedition to capture this fragile beauty, I soon noticed how almost nothing is under my control - not the weather, not the shape of the icebergs, not how they float, not their colours. You only have your camera under control. After a 12 hours of sailing on a day which was mostly overcast, we found a little leak in the clouds near the horizon and the entire bay turned orange. It looked beautiful, but I needed the contrast - I knew something was missing until I saw this little piece of ice floating around, more than half submerged. I took as many as 650 frames but none of them worked in my head, it was a split second decision to go compose the scene vertically. I got the alignment correct, the iceberg perfectly in the frame, and the red sails illuminated with the rays of sun, reflecting in the water - and the midnight sun, in all its glory.

metadata.name: A tribute to All Stars
metadata.description: I’ve been wanting to tokenize a commemorative skull as a tribute to NBA Top Shot for quite some time, but I had been waiting for the right opportunity. When the All-Star game was announced I knew there would be a good chance that an All-Star pack would emerge, and I decided to wait until then to create this artwork. Why the All-Star pack in particular? To me it has a double meaning, first celebrating the incredibly talented players in the game that came together in this very special weekend, but I also wanted to use this as an opportunity to celebrate the Top Shot team for leading the way for the collectibles industry. Very few times in our lifetime we are able to observe when a disruptive idea, vision, partnership orchestration and execution come together so swiftly to disrupt and industry and set the stage for how things will be done moving forward. I think this exactly what we are just witnessing, and I am excited to be part of it as a collector. Hope the new owner of this skull enjoys it as much as I enjoyed making it.  3500 x 3500 pixels. Rendered at 350 pixels per inch.

creator.bio: Emily Edelman is an artist exploring the intricacies of text, typography, scale, and color theory through her innovative generative algorithms and unique digital practices. With a classically trained eye and a taste for quiet complexity, her work examines the thin line between legibility and abstraction within the rich and timeless realm of letterforms and visual communication.  \n\nEmily’s work has been shown internationally with Art Blocks, Bright Moments, Verse Works, theVERSEverse, Artsy, EXPANDED.ART, VellumLA, and Ames Yavuz Gallery. She graduated from the Rhode Island School of Design in 2012.

metadata.name: Voluminous
metadata.description:
Voluminous" delves into the multifaceted nature of truth in physical objects, particularly books. By presenting an unedited grid of sequential photographs, it encapsulates the entirety of a book in a single moment, revealing more than any singular angle or image could. This found compilation, initially intended to discover the book's best perspective, instead offers a deeper reflection: the way a book is read and understood can shift over time, depending on the lens through which it’s viewed. Perspective plays a pivotal role in its appreciation, as each revisit of the book can reveal something different. "Voluminous" flattens and animates the time and space of a book’s experience, challenging conventional understanding of objects and their inherent truths. It collapses the typically unseen, time-based nature of a book into a visible, instantaneous form.


This work continues the artist’s study of typography, examining the interplay between legibility, abstraction, and the evolving experience of reading. However, this time, instead of using generative algorithms, the artist turns to the physical object and photography to explore this dynamic. By focusing on the materiality of books and their representation through sequential images, Edelman delves into how the evolving experience of reading is shaped by both the tangible and the visual. Her approach maintains the delicate balance between quiet complexity and visual clarity, now extending beyond digital processes to examine the relationship between physical form and perception.

metadata.description: We all have a place our minds drift when we close our eyes. For some it’s the comfort of being right at home. Others let their imagination run wild, sweeping them off to distant lands. Far too often we awake to the sad reality that our dreams are nothing more than fantasies. So close to touch, yet seemingly unreachable. What’s holding us back from breaking through that barrier and searching for those places? I had seen this vision for years, never knowing our journeys would inevitably cross paths. On this day, I found the gift I had dreamed of my whole life. A reminder that we could all use a little more love in our lives. Now it's your turn to close your eyes. Your escape is only one dream away.

metadata.name: The Four Seasons
metadata.description:
The Four Seasons
It is an exercise of progressive evolution of generative art (GenArt2.0) with variants of forms and colors to represent the four seasons of the year.
This is my genesis drop in SuperRare, so I decided to gather the four artworks in one, to turn it into a 1/1 that is, at the same time, four illustrations in one.

GenArt2.0. A generative art alternative
Unlike more traditional forms of generative art, this collection is not based on one code specifically designed to produce lines or curves but is the result of mixing various algorithms where each produces a predetermined type of graphics. The enormous advantage of this system is an infinity of variables that can be intermingled to give surprising results, always maintaining that wonderful characteristic of generative art of achieving unpredictable and surprising results. Welcome to GenArt2

metadata.name: The Whale's Mouth
metadata.description:
"The Whale's Mouth" is the first piece in my "Atmøs" NFT collection. This is a series celebrating the elements in full flow. How small we all are in comparison. I love nothing more than capturing their unrestrained might at work. 

This particular piece depicts a rare "Whale's Mouth" cloud formation/thunderstorm that threw lightning bolts, fierce winds, tornadoes, and hailstones the size of tennis balls across the East Coast of the US in 2018. 

One of nature's most alarming and astonishing phenomena, the shelf cloud looming over Manhattan, is often referred to as a 'whale's mouth' since the dark and ribbed formations give the impression of being consumed by a giant whale. 

Regarded as one of the most severe weather events on record to pass through the Hudson Valley, I wanted this NFT to expand on the story depicted in the image. Using the token itself as a canvas, I have inscribed details associated with the storm (pooled from news & area weather reports from the area) into the metadata.

This link that NFT's provide, a blend between art and technology, forms a unique record of a rare atmospheric event now immortalised on the blockchain.

metadata.name: Oasis
metadata.description: As human’s we have a strong natural connection to the ocean, it’s forces are a symbol of power and tranquillity which energizes and revitalises our soul. Growing up in Sydney, Australia, my lifestyle has always been synonymous with the ocean and the beach culture that surrounds it. The feeling of the warm sun on your skin, the smell of the salt water, and the sound of the waves crashing. All feelings that I hope to portray through this series of work titled “Ocean Canvas”. As an early pioneer of aerial photography I began capturing the oceans of Australia as my main subject matter in early 2016, treating the water below as the paint to my canvas. The thing that drew me most to the ocean was it’s unpredictability and forever changing nature. With the ocean constantly in motion it creates an abstract sense of reality when viewed from above, painting different textures, colours and emotions as each second passes. This image was captured at Mona Vale Beach in Sydney's North Shore and depicts the power of the ocean from a perspective that allows us to appreciate it in it’s full capacity and witness the artistic collaboration betwen man kind and mother nature.

metadata.name: The Scholars
metadata.description:
In the courtyard of the Chinguetti mosque, two scholars engage in conversation, embodying the essence of tradition and cultural preservation. This image captures the timeless significance of preserving our worldwide cultural heritage.
Chinguetti holds historical importance as a former hub for Saharan trade routes, connecting North Africa with sub-Saharan regions. As a center for trade caravans, it facilitated the exchange of goods, ideas, and knowledge across vast desert landscapes. Additionally, Chinguetti served as a religious and scholarly center, with its renowned mosque housing valuable manuscripts and libraries, attracting pilgrims and scholars from afar.

metadata.name: Machinic Situatedness 3
metadata.description: Machinic Situatedness is a series of artworks that uses artificial intelligence (A.I) to explore the subject 'cultural situatedness' and influences in the genre of AI art. These works are created by an A.I drawing inspiration from Budhhist painting styles to create an abstract, dream like output using GANs. This series asks the questions- what is an AI machine’s cultural underpinning and how can we broaden its scope? These draw a lot of reference from Nam June Paik's TV Buddha work, here alluding to the cycles of transcendence which we undergo as a species, continuous cycles of trying to become something more than ourselves, which we are now channeling through the evolving role of AI in our lives and AI itself is by learning more from us.

metadata.name: Incredible India - Ooty
metadata.description:
Located in the Nilgiri Hills in Southern India, it is known as the "Queen of Western Ghaats” – a mountain range that stretches over 1600 kms along the western coast of the Indian peninsula. Originally occupied by the Tribals, it came under the rule of the British East India Company by the treaty in 1799.

Serving as the summer capital of the Madras Presidency - an administrative subdivision of British India; it was visited by British officials during the colonial days as a popular summer getaway.

In 1819, John Sullivan, then Collector of the region, visited this hill resort and was enthralled by the beauty of the place. In a letter to Sir Thomas Munro, the Governor of Madras, Sullivan wrote "it resembles Switzerland, more than any country of Europe... the hills beautifully wooded and fine strong spring with running water in every valley.”

In 1882, Snooker originated on the billiard tables of a local Club, invented by Sir Neville Chamberlain, rules of which were also first drafted and codified by Neville himself. 

Today, as a popular hill resort, its economy is based on tourism and agriculture, along with the manufacture of medicines and photographic film.

This, is Ooty.

metadata.name: Grace
metadata.description:
There is an ancient belief that during the active labor mother, while in a state of weakened frontal brain cortex (a state of deep meditation and deactivated consciousness), “goes to the stars after her baby’s spirit and they come back together”. 

This is my wife’s portrait breastfeeding our two month old baby. I could feel the bonding between them and capturing that moment made me feel bonded with them too.
She then started singing lullaby to soothe him, which made that moment even more precious and complete. 

It was a lullaby based on “Cradle Song”, a poem by Rafael Patkanian (1830-1892), one of the most popular Armenian poets. 
A lullaby with powerful underlying meaning and beautifully simple melody which speaks to all Armenians as a whole. 

Nightingale, leave our garden, 
Where soft dews the blossoms steep ; 
With thy litanies melodious  
Come and sing my son to sleep!  
Nay, he sleeps not for thy chanting,  
And his weeping hath not ceased.  
Come not, nightingale ! My darling  
Does not wish to be a priest.  
O thou thievish, clever jackdaw,  
That in coin findest thy joy,  
With thy tales of gold and profit  
Come and soothe my wailing boy !  
Nay, thy chatter does not lull him,  
And his crying is not stayed.  
Come not, jackdaw ! for my darling  
Will not choose the merchant’s trade.  
Wild dove, leave the fields and pastures  
Where thou grievest all day long;  
Come and bring my boy sweet slumber  
With thy melancholy song !  
Still he weeps. Nay, come not hither,  
Plaintive songster, for I see  
That he loves not lamentations,  
And no mourner will he be.  
Leave thy chase, brave-hearted falcon!  
Haply he thy song would hear.  
And the boy lay hushed, and slumbered,  
With the war-notes in his ear.

English translation of the poem by Rafael Patkanian. 

Photo taken by medium format black&white film.
Yerevan, Armenia,  2020.

« It’s good to leave each day behind, like flowing water, free from sadness.
Yesterday is gone, and it’s tale told. Today new seeds are growing » .

I set up a dreamy decor , with plants and flowers, vases and embroidered backgrounds on a golden sand, with a blue sky and waves cascading around .
I then started to capture the natural grace of these elegant Twin Mamas. 
And other enchanting People. 

Traveling all along the Sahel, from Senegal to Ethiopia to meet the communities who are living along the Great Green Wall, I saw the beauty, the pain & the hopes of a continent I adore.
Africa. On this incredible journey across the Sahara, my camera was my loyal companion, the People were my light.

Created By Nilus J. Dantes.

Rorschach's is a collection of Surrealist, Mirrored Abstract photography that uses tenants of the Psychological Ink Blot test as part of its foundation & are derived from various photos within my Photography portfolio. Each Photograph is designed to invoke subjective imagery based on the perception, imagination and psychological perspective of each individual holder.

The intent behind Rorschach's is to challenge the notion that Art and collectables within the Web3 space & beyond require a complex story or a deeply nuanced narrative in order to have value. Instead every Rorschach allows for each individual viewing it to form their own story, meaning and emotional connection with it. Like a great deal of Art this allows for potentially endless possibilities but on a larger and more distinctive scale.

Rorschach's are photographs but more than that they're photos that are morphed, mirrored and transformed into unique 1/1 piece's of abstract photography. Each Rorschach is determined by the elements held within the original photo which are derived from an infinite number of traits from water, bone, skin, light, shadow, people & more.

Rorschach's are minted on their own ERC-721 Smart contract through Manifold.

metadata.name: Steve
metadata.description:
Steve and a picket fence taken looking south over Berg Lake and Mount Robson.  What is Steve?  Most have never heard of it, those that have might just think it's an Aurora.  Let me explain below why this purple ribbon is so special and why this shot is so important.  

This extremely rare capture went viral, and became a piece of scientific history.  The shot and associated time-lapse were then used in the physics paper led by W. E. Archer, “The Vertical Distribution of the Optical Emissions of a Steve and Picket Fence Event” published in Geophysical Research Letters, of which I was a co-author. The paper advanced our understanding of these phenomena by narrowing down the altitude extent of the Steve and picket fence emissions. 

Steve is east/west mid latitude phenomena at lower latitudes than you typically see an Aurora. The phenomenon is thought to be caused by a 25 km (16 mi) wide ribbon of hot plasma at 3000C (5430F) flowing at a speed of 6 km/s (3.7 mi/s) (compared to 10 m/s (33 ft/s) outside the ribbon).

While occurring with the same space weather that brings an Aurora; Steve is not an aurora as it is not generated through the precipitation of electrons.  Steve runs East/West while an Auroral beams runs North/South along the magnetitic field lines to the poles.

For unknown reasons Steve is sometimes but not always associated with a picket fence aurora.  The picket fence always forms below Steve and is thought to be caused by electron precipitation.  While Steve has been witnessed and documented by the aurora chasing community for a long time it is only newly being characterised by the science community. 

The paper advanced our understanding of these phenomena by narrowing down the altitude extent of the Steve and picket fence emissions.

I was fortunate enough to capture Steve while observing to the South while at Berg Lake.  This meant I was clear of any interference from the Aurora to the North.    The image was taken while camping overnight at 1,646m (5,400 ft) as part of a multi-day back country hike. 

The North Face of Mt Robson rises 2,308m (7,572ft) out of the lake to a total height of 3,954 m (12,972 ft) and is the highest peak in the Canadian Rockies.

metadata.name: BUILDING BLOCKS
metadata.description:
“BUILDING BLOCKS” — This piece is the first in a small series of photos that touches on my love for capturing extreme perspectives through aerial photography. Perspectives rarely seen by the human eye.

A nod to the unique work of architect Ildefons Cerdà, this photograph looks down onto the Eixample district of Barcelona where his cleverly designed grind pattern of blocks stand like a puzzle below. As you enter Barcelona by plane, the blocks are hard to miss, a huge area and perfectly uniform - it had always intrigued me. In 2019 I began the tedious planning of an open-door helicopter flight over the city, the goal of course was to capture this iconic landscape. After a few months of coordination, permits and aligning on the best time of year for the perfect light, I took to the skies in March and spent a perfect sunset capturing what would become one of my most iconic images. 

The collector will receive a gallery quality, signed and framed physical print of the image alongside the NFT. The size of the print will be determined by the collector and Jacob once the sale is complete.

metadata.name: Awakening
metadata.description:
-Awakening-

Part 1 of 3 in my “Self Discovery” series that revolves around how I’ve grown to be the photographer that I am today. 

"It is possible to experience an awakening in this life through realising just how precious each moment in time truly is."

In the last year after returning home to New Zealand from the United States, I realised that nothing brings me more joy than being out in nature and capturing the kind of beauty that words can’t adequately describe. My most recent road trip around the South Island was a defining moment in my photography career as I pushed my creative boundaries further.
 
This image was taken from a magical sunrise moment in New Zealand, in an area of the country that often sees a lot of rain and clouds. This trip was my first chance to explore after being in lockdown for over 4 months. The forecast for the west coast wasn’t looking good but I took a risk and drove 5 hours to get to the location knowing there was a chance there wouldn’t be a sunrise at all. On this particular morning, I felt overwhelmed with the show the sky put on in front of me. Lenticular clouds, low mist on the lake, and a spectacular view of the iconic Mount Cook and Mount Tasman piercing the skyline. Sunrises like this make me believe in the magic of everyday life. There's beauty all around us if we choose to see our surroundings with open eyes. 

Here I was sitting in silence, every problem and every doubt disappearing as the beauty around me helped calm the chaos in my mind. This was such a sweet feeling after being confined in the city of Auckland for so long. To be out on the road with no plans and a camera in my hands made me feel in tune with myself again.

I knew the moment of awakening began when I chose to embrace all that I am and that every single moment is precious. Now is the time to chase your dreams and believe in yourself.

metadata.name: Diamond High
metadata.description:
I visited Iceland in 2020 to highlight the issues of global warming and ever shrinking glaciers. I struggled to convey the sheer size and magnitude of the giant ice boulders that had broken away from the Jökulsárlón glacier from the ground as they are washed back onto the shore. Taking to the skies allowed me to more adequately portray quite how large the area was. The ice blocks often weighing tones stretch for a couple of miles along the aptly named Diamond beach. My aim was to produce a captivating body of work that would capture people’s attention and make them look deeper into the issues of global warming that affect us all. From the set of images, I produced this was the most powerful and impacting image, even being highly commended in the Nat Geo Landscape photographer of the year competition in 2021.

metadata.name: Shaken, Not Stirred
metadata.description:
Have you ever seen an image that you couldn't shake? Something so wild, so incredible, it writes itself into permanent memory?

That's how I felt when I first saw this sea stack.

The thing was... there wasn't geotags or GPS coordinates. 

No "how-to" guide books. 

No Top-10 websites. 

Just hours on Google Earth... scouring maps and trying to imagine what the lo-fi topographic images looked like in real life.

Then came hundreds of miles of windy coastal roads. 

First stop was a dud. 

Second had no way down to the water. 

It was the third stop when I walked up to this glorious pile of rock and The Matterhorn was found.

Scrambling down the cliff, I excitedly pulled out my camera and broke the #1 rule - I turned my back to the water and a sneaker wave came, knocking me and my tripod down almost taking us out to sea.

Shaken, not stirred, I picked myself up, shuffled up the cliffside, and watched as the horizon broke open and this spectacular sky lit up before me.  
------  
*Shaken, Not Stirred was originally minted on August 29th, 2021 to the OG Foundation Contract and has been migrated to The Art of Jordan Inglee 1/1 Manifold Contract

metadata.name: Dissolution of Eternity
metadata.description:
The Jokulsarlon Glacier Lagoon under fiery light, in its endless cadence of moving pieces of ice, teaches us lessons of impermanence and dissolution.

This place is quite simply the most beautiful work of moving art I've ever seen in my short life…

The landscapes in Iceland are not only majestic, but also extremely volatile. After being on so many unstable places, which would never look the same on a second visit, I found the king of pure Buddhist impermanence: the Jokulsarlon glacier lagoon and its endless floating delicate sculptures of ice, right in front of my eyes.
 
On this afternoon I had the honor of witnessing an epic, extremely rare and incredibly intense sunset in this location, with enough time to choose from the endless possible angles and compositions. Due to the fast-moving pieces of ice, doing a long exposure in this place would be impossible, as details would get lost and become blurred, so I had to act quick while choosing the composition.

Since the ice pieces were a few meters away from the shore and I wanted to get the classic immersive look of wide angle lenses, I had to enter into the almost frozen water to get really close to the ice and create this image. 

From time to time, chunks of melted ice would meet my legs with great subtlety, making me feel like a part of the whole moving scenario. Unforgettable...

-------

One of my most iconic and famous images. It was published on a double page spread of National Geographic printed Magazine, featured in the official Sony Alpha Universe website along with an interview about its creation, exhibited in large format print in Lisbon individual exhibition and has been featured in dozens of other media channels.  

10% of profits will be donated to Reforestation Project Give Back to Nature. 

Large format physical print available upon request by the collector.

metadata.name: Salt and Dust
metadata.description:
Salt and dust, breathing atop a rock that doesn’t know your name. 
Heart filled with fuel and lungs filled with fire—
What will you say before your breath runs out? 

Living like raindrops: born from chaos, racing towards a certain end. 
A ripple in a sea of strangers who blink and forget we lived at all. 
If the fall is all we have... how will you have it sound? 

There is purpose to be found here—
Moments of peace, freedom and power.
Moments that make us who we are.

We are fragile, like lights dancing across the sky, thinking the sun will never come.
Yet, in spite of our frailty and the heavy wheel of time…
Once, we are young,
And we are brave. 

There is beauty in believing we can make a difference, no matter how the hourglass runs dry.
Our hearts light fires in the lighthouses, shining through storms for those who come after us.
Do not mistake this moment for forever. 

What kind of light will you leave?

metadata.name: Memory Palace
metadata.description:
This digital painting communicates the aesthetics of memory storage and recall in my mind's eye.  Each container is an "address" for information, with size denoting the broadness of the category. It is like an inverted box treemap, where the contents exist on the outside of the parent container. The sum of their volumes never exceeds that of the base they're attached to. 
It is difficult to accurately capture the forms, as specifics are impossible due to the transient nature of the mind's eye. The space is somewhere between 2D and 3D, with motion somehow gluing the two together. Such is the nature of synesthesia/ideasthesia.

metadata.description: “The law is the law” means your argument has no way of winning because the law has already determined that your argument has no merit. No matter how outdated/outrageous/ridiculous/stupid a rule (or law) might be, too bad.. because it's the law. While littering is a problem, a bigger problem is when the ones enforcing the law apply it literally, as if their human judgement is not needed. If that is the case, we can use robots now. In this drawing, we can clearly see that the shipwrecked man is trying to throw a SOS bottle into the ocean. However now he must be punished by the law, because it doesn't matter if his act was a last attempt to save his own life, the law is clear against littering, and so the police man now makes sure it is being applied.

Growing up in the Bay Area, I always dreamt of experiencing the snow days that I saw in movies. I had so much envy for the kids that got to stay home from school, build snowmen, and have snowball fights. 

During the pandemic, I moved out of California, and headed north to Seattle, WA. My first winter living here, I finally got to wake up to snow. I was overjoyed, and couldn't wait to wander and experience snow where I lived for the first time.

This year, it snowed again on Christmas night. The next morning, I went out and explored with my camera. I came across this vintage Mercedes and loved how the iconic hood ornament was mostly submerged in snow.

metadata.name: The Winding Path
metadata.description:
It was a bright, sunny and a pleasant day for a stroll, even thought the air was surprisingly cold. The Winding Path stretched along the ancient walls and the nature had reclaimed most of it. The place was so quiet that the hero could only hear her own footsteps. With every step she took, the reflective stairs revealed a glimpse of the countless possible futures. The fleeting images conveyed more of a mood rather than visions.

Overall the future appeared vibrant and hopeful which was encouraging. However, the hero noted a few elusive dark shadows that dashed across the glass surface. She also noticed that the more she focused on that darkness - the more it grew. It was a hypnotic pull that she had to consciously break off, even though she really wanted to know more about it. Walking this fine line, she peered deeper into the glimmering reflections, letting her intuition guide her. Assembling the many tiny fragments to make sense of it all.

metadata.name: Rhyolite
metadata.description:
"Rhyolite" is the second piece of Johan Lolos' genesis NFT series "Hálendið". Hálendið, which means "Highlands" in Icelandic, is a photography series of 5 pieces that are a tribute to the painting-like landscapes of the Icelandic Highlands. 
Ever since Johan first visited this part of the world he has been amazed by the huge diversity of colors that can be found in the Highlands. The unparalleled contrasts and shapes of the Hálendið make this region of Iceland so unique in the whole world, to the point it becomes almost surreal to any visitor. 

This particular piece is an aerial image of Landmannalaugar, shot from a Cessna aircraft. While the dominant color of this photo clearly is brown/orange, one can observe the patches of light green spread across the image. This is due to the high amount of rhyolite, which the mountains of this area are made of.  Rhyolite is an extrusive igneous rock, formed from magma rich in silica that is extruded from a volcanic vent to cool quickly on the surface rather than slowly in the subsurface. Most rhyolites are uniform in texture, and their color ranges from gray to light-pink, depending on the striations made by the lava flow. Natural rhyolite displays green, cream and occasional brown tones with patterns and inclusions.

The very first time Johan went to Landmannalaugar, he just couldn't believe his eyes. Was it real? Was it a dream? Why did those mountains look like paintings? His obsession with the Icelandic Highlands never stopped, to the point that they became his favorite landscapes to photograph.

** The collector will receive a gallery quality, signed and framed physical print of the image alongside the NFT. The print will be delivered with a certificate of authenticity.

metadata.name: A perception of Hell
metadata.description:
This painting was created through a system of symbols that are often self-referential, combining geometric elements and organic forms with a deep love for art history.

With A perception of Hell I explore imagined spaces tied to moments of transition between the material and the spiritual. To build this world, I draw from many sources: gestures from my own practice, references from modern art, and visual elements inspired by works of Bosch  which I reinterpreted using traditional techniques.

I abstracted forms, colors, and symbols to propose a new visual narrative. My intention is to transform the experience of the original works with a contemporary perspective.

The images, characters, and symbols in the painting dissolve, merge, and shift just like their meanings. As a woman artist, I find it meaningful to revisit and reinterpret works by male masters from a time when women were not allowed to study painting, adding my own voice and point of view.

The result is a complex composition where geometric and organic forms coexist, made entirely by hand using oil on canvas. 
Through this work, I invite reflection on the evolution of society, spirituality, and the human condition today.
This token includes the physical painting for its first collector
Oil on Canvas

metadata.name: To Withstand
metadata.description:
These “Mindscapes” comprise my genesis NFT series. I seek to explore inner worlds rather than attempt to capture the elusive reality of Nature. These designs translate perfect moments of clarity and stillness into a language made of color. In the blink of a mind's eye, they reflect the exact moments of heightened pleasure, silence, purity, conscious awakening, balance and alignment we all strive for. I conceive of these lands as utopias - a place you dream to live in and can achieve only within the vast expanse of yourself.

For every oil painting I make, I go through a creative process of designing a preliminary sketch digitally, mapping out the composition and the color scheme, and ultimately landing on a final finished digital piece before a brush ever touches the canvas. Up until now, these pieces have been private and not for sale. With this release, however, you can now own a piece of the intimate process through which I create work.

From this lofty view, amidst the darkness and downpour, the interconnectedness of life becomes hauntingly clear. It's a humbling experience that thrives amid adversity, urging me to learn, grow, and move forward.

I find solace and inspiration in this atmospheric convergence of art and introspection. The rain-soaked rooftop becomes a canvas where the drops create an ethereal dance, adding a touch of melancholy and mystery to my creations. From this heightened perspective, I expand my knowledge, nourish my spirit, and propel myself forward along the winding path of my creative journey, guided by the subtle beauty of the darkest storms.

metadata.name: Folia #1
metadata.description:
The word Folia comes from "follie" wich means madness in French. In Brazil, the term is used to refer to the festive crowd at Carnival time. The generative art project Folia alludes to the image of this mad and mixed crowd seen from above.

Carnival is marked by diversity and playful uses of the streets. In several Brazilian regions, thousands of people of all ages and types occupy public spaces in a crazy mix of joy, music, fantasy, colors and celebration. 

The images created in Folia series also refer to the confetti and the profusion of lights, movement and colors of the party. From macro to micro, the images can even refer to the details of the carnival costumes with their shiny sequins. Folia has two sub-series: Night and Day. 

This project is part of the Carnavale exhibition, curated by Selkie in partnership with SuperRare.

metadata.name: The Last Resort
metadata.description:
Welcome, welcome to our park!
A place on earth of grand remark!
Where every single stone was sculpted,
and growth is never interrupted.

Welcome to the land of eve!
Where buzzing coolers hide the screams
of all the paramedic teams
in this place once built for dreams.

Come and play! Attractions are plenty,
each one designed to take every last penny.
Welcome to distraction land!
The place that feeds from your demand.

So many colors compete for your sight:
lights and music, left and right.
A land where there’s no quiet at night,
but If you look closely things ain't so bright.

Only scratches on the surface but fisuras deep inside.
This foundation, oh mamita, hundred per cent about to crack.
Like a house of humid cards, a rather weak and feeble stack,
only held up by some speeches, all as sound as quack quack quack.

Something's rotten in this park.
Something growing, something dark.
If you ever see some cracks: fill them up! Fill them up!
Fill them with… glue! Corazón que no siente… Dont leave a clue!
Impending doom? None to be seen.
Cassandra was payed off by some magazine

Something flows in this made up town.
It ain't the water cause water so brown
from all the children that ever have drowned
(even Ofelias in their wedding gowns).

You wanted happy, and you wanted fast,
dismissing the steps to make those things last.
We are building for newness, let's go in a blast.
We won't be the ones building a past.

You wanna be fly, you wanna nonstop,
and while you are distracted with circus and chalk,
we´ll take your wallet, your shoes, and your clock.
Even our candy is sponsored by Glock!


Kings are the ones who own a fast pass ,
but how can you be royal when you are one from the mass?
If you pay enough we might let you kill!
Pushing the buttons that your soul might fill.
It’s all for the rush, all in order to feel.
Do you sign here? Do we have a deal?

Under terms and conditions 
you'll find  ammunition but please…

Stay seated for this ride.
We can't guarantee you'll get to come back.

Fill in the void, with love made of plaster.
Don’t mix with water or else big disaster.
And once you are full, the rug we will pull,
we were always a wolf dressed as a bull:

Instant gratification.
Instagram deification.
Chasing after ghost sensations,
skipping all the conversations.
We don't talk 'bout the situation.
We don't need no education.
Just some big people concentration,
and, of course, passive participation.

A horde of zombies, here they come!
Footsteps loud as martial drums.
Humming voices fill the air,
not a single flying care.
To be fair they are not aware
that war has already been declared,
by beings displaced due unfair treaties,
paid with sorrow and half baked pities.

How much did you burn to earn your plot of land?
How much are you growing in your fields made of sand?
Always for the conquest shouting: let's expand the brand!
But did you ever stop to just look around? Did you ever notice, now, where you stand?

King of the desert, left no home for other souls.
Poured gasoline down through every single hole.
“People like explosions, those score higher on the polls!”
There’s no love in las caricias of a hand that all controls.

Organic ain’t attractive, at least not all of the time,
if not ask the caterpillar while its hanging upside down.
You can build a reputation with no need to have a crown.
It is better to be no-one than to be an evil clown.

Down there the mutants, the ones cast away, their new life is forming with other ways.
Stunted by cold, in shadow they grew,
like moss and like ivy, like thick stew and flu.
In sewers they built  their underground town:
all that were born, were born deaf and blind.
But learning the language of slow tactile gestures,
 they created a tongue of lyrics and questions.

"Why are we down here? Who put us here?
We are children of greed and hate's our seed.
This is our land and spring we demand!
We may not have bones but together we stand!"

A landing place, a quay.
An army of rats in patient array.
Coming at you, with the promise of fame,
all that ass kissing has made your mouth lame!
A knot in the throat, a stiff stone for tongue,
you could never utter the words “I belong”.

metadata.name: amorphous erosion
metadata.description:
White Pocket Arizona has been an area that in the last 6 years I have become absolutely obsessed with. I’ve kept coming back here every year since my first visit in 2016, and most recently staying for days on end. I love any landscape that takes you to a different place, a place that doesn’t feel like earth while you’re there. White Pocket is a remote single square mile of eroded sandstone accessed by long stretches of deep, soft sand in the middle of the desert, in the middle of absolutely nothing. Geologists don’t know what created this place; some geologist proclaim that this is a result of "soft sedimentation" in the Jurassic period, while some believe it was the result of a huge sand-slide mass triggered by an earthquake.

This fall of 2022, I headed out to the desert from the Pacific Northwest with Hollis and my Two dogs. After a few days on the road we arrived to my favorite road in the world, House Rock Road. This road is literally a portal to so many areas of other-worldly landscape- from the Wave to the longest slot canyon in the world- it’s a photographer’s and geology lovers paradise. 

The road up to white Pocket isn’t for the faint of heart. Deep sand pockets that require a 4x4 with high clearance, and some desert navigating skills. A challenging yet rewarding drive results in an impressive, otherworldly desert expanse of extraordinary geology. We came out with a plan to stay a couple days, but ended up at White Pocket for 5 days as the weather wasn’t what I had hoped for. Catching the tail end of monsoon season, I had hoped for some storms that could bring killer clouds for sunset. 

On the last day after getting nothing but clear skies and bright sun, a storm rolled in. At sunset, the skies were a dark vivid blue, and all of a sudden lit up like crazy, vibrant pinks, purples and oranges, and lasted for only a good 2 minutes- it was quick. This image was a short moment that I captured in the middle of this quick yet insanely stunning sunset- no blending or heavy editing techniques- this was truly the scene. 

As the sound of thunder became closer and closer and the sky began to fade, I packed up my gear and hustled back out to our truck- the storm was getting intense and lightning was right over us. After witnessing one of the most beautiful sunsets I’ve ever seen, we drove out followed by the most captivating desert storm. Truly of of the best days I’ve had that I will never forget.

metadata.name: THE ORIGIN OF LIFE
metadata.description:
The origin of life on Earth began over 3 billion years ago, evolving from the most basic of microbes into a great variety of complex organisms over time. 

Just like animals, volcanoes go through a life cycle of birth, growth, decline, and finally death, just like people (they become extinct). Despite their reputation as destructive forces, volcanoes actually were essential for the development of life on the planet. Volcanic eruptions helped release most of Earth's water from the crust and mantle. They also led to the formation of the Earth's atmosphere. In addition to water and air, volcanoes took a big part in the formation of land, another necessity for many life forms. But what role does humanity carry in itself for this planet?

The collection "I am planet Earth" aims to remind each of us that we are all part of this planet and that by destroying everything around us, we are destroying ourselves.

metadata.name: Cine & Color: Just Us
metadata.description: There was a sense of magic in the air as a fleeting snowstorm endlessly raged throughout the city while most New Yorkers stayed home. Without a soul in sight, the ground became an undisturbed blanket of white, powdery snow. Usually dried and leafless around this time, the trees calmly stood adorned in frost, giving an eerie, magical feeling. As far as I could see, everything was lost in the storm's presence. It was then when I saw you, seemingly appearing out of thin air, just you and your puppy walking serenely in the snow, unbothered by the winds and untouched by the cold. For you, I could tell this moment was special; a rare opportunity to have the park for yourself, to enjoy the music of the wind and the silence of the day. It was just us.

metadata.name: The Swan Lake
metadata.description:
On the seventh night, the sky was clear, and the moon was in its last quarter.

The woman was still missing  her lost heart, her very essence, that had taken the form of a pear. It disappeared so long ago, she could hardly remember what it was like when it was hers. She had heard that the day before, a lamentation of swans had seen her heart at the bottom of the lake. 

The woman couldn’t find the swans, but she hoped if she willed them, they would appear and show her the way.

Her eyes closed. The sound of water echoed the room…//

II. The Swan Lake // This artwork is specifically created for the genesis series “In the shadows”, and is based on Tale of the Blue Pear, a multi-awarded series about a woman who has lost her heart, and enters a parallel universe, hoping to find it again. There she finds talking animals, strange characters, giant beings, superpowers, as well as her own alter egos.
With subtle elements of allegory and irony, this visual story raises questions about our own lives and the never-ending quest for inner peace and happiness.

metadata.name: Alpine Dreams
metadata.description: Some of Europe's most beautiful alpine mountains, lakes, and meadows can be found in the Dolomites, which are located in northern Italy. The Dolomites are simply my favorite region in all of Europe and I make sure to visit every summer! While exploring the Odle/Geisler peaks, I came across this breathtaking scene. Can you imagine having a cabin with a view such as this!? I loved this location so much that I set up my tent and enjoyed these views all night and into the next morning. This image is one of my top-performing photos on social media. To me, this image captures the feeling/spirit of summer, as well as the Dolomites in general, and bottles up that feeling! Every time I view this image it brings me right back to this magical moment in time!

metadata.name: Influencers
metadata.description: Influencer culture has reshaped our perception of reality, blending curated personas with aspirational content that distorts authenticity. By amplifying a selective, idealized version of life, influencers create a feedback loop where followers strive for unattainable lifestyles, perpetuating cycles of consumerism and comparison. The curated facade often masks the emotional labor and artifice involved, leaving viewers disconnected from genuine human experiences. In becoming arbiters of what’s desirable, influencers have turned personal narratives into branded commodities, warping the boundary between reality and spectacle.

metadata.name: A night in Chelsea
metadata.description: As a photographer I carry my camera with me almost at all times, specially when walking around the streets of New York. One night after grabbing a few drinks with friends around the Meatpacking District, I saw this scene, that felt like it was just from another time. I immediately grabbed my camera and took the shot. The lady walking by The Old Homestead Steakhouse gave it a wonderful nostalgia that felt like autumn in New York in the 40s. I used a haze filter on my lens that gave the glow on the neon signs, and then adjusted the colors to make it more cinematic. This part of NYC is great for pictures, specially around Chelsea, and this particular business is the oldest continuously operating steakhouse in the United States.

metadata.name: Episode I: Spotlight
metadata.description:
This heavenly light shows down upon the Manhattan Beach Pier between thunderstorms. 

Just as it had been written thousands of years ago…the legends foretold of a Pierfect day in October. Thunder, Lightning, followed by a golden light that would bring a great swell to Los Angeles. So it has been written so it shall be. 

Of the 20 years I've lived here and 15 of those years as a professional photographer taking photos of the Pier, I have never seen it like this. These unique and special moments are exactly what I point to when I'm asked "How can you take so many photos of the same place?". It is never the same place, always changing with the angle of the sun, the direction and intensity of the swell, the Pier People who inhabit it.

My Genesis SuperRare Series image begins our journey and your opportunity to give into Pier Pressure.

metadata.name: Aesthetics of Eroticism#5
metadata.description:
How much prejudice and restriction do we live with? The world is changing faster than I thought, but we always try to categorize everything within our framework. Can AI really not feel any warmth? I want to reflect on the aesthetics of eroticism that AI creates.

Prejudice and restriction are pervasive in our society, and they often prevent us from fully embracing the changes and diversity that the world has to offer. While the world is evolving at an unprecedented pace, we tend to cling to our biases and preconceptions, making it difficult to fully appreciate the complexity of our surroundings.

As for AI, while it may not possess the same kind of emotions and subjective experiences as humans, it is capable of producing remarkably sophisticated and nuanced works of art. This includes the aesthetics of eroticism, which can be explored and expressed through AI-generated content.

AI-generated aesthetics of eroticism can take many forms, from simple visual images to complex narratives and interactive experiences. While some may argue that these creations lack the warmth and human touch of traditional forms of art, others believe that they offer unique insights and perspectives on human sexuality and desire.

metadata.name: Transcendentia Primordialis
metadata.description:
Transcendentia Primordialis is a hypnotic mixed media painting that evokes the moment consciousness emerges from chaos. At its core is a dense black vortex, radiating concentric waves of fine, hand-drawn linework that ripple outward like energy fields or ancient memory maps. These lines—both ordered and turbulent—trace the evolution of form from formlessness, inviting the viewer into a meditative engagement with the work.  
Throughout the composition, soft washes of acrylic color and layered collage elements pierce the monochrome landscape—gestures of material birth from energetic origins. Coral pinks, deep blues, moss greens, and gold drift through the piece like fragments of spirit, memory, and biology, suggesting the formation of sentient matter from primordial ether.  
The painting’s surface shimmers with dimensional depth: textural collage pieces and reflective moments embedded into the canvas mirror the multiplicity of experience. It is at once microscopic and cosmic—an interior map of becoming, as well as a celestial field of potential.  
A visual meditation on emergence, dissolution, and the cyclical nature of being, Transcendentia Primordialis invites viewers to contemplate their own origin stories—both ancient and ongoing.  

Original physical mixed media painting, 30" x 40", is available on primary with request. 

Curated for One Love Art DAO with NFT AOTEAROA for Sacred Origins at the Jutta Gallery in New York City, June 25, 2025.

metadata.name: Refracted Dreams
metadata.description:
I have always loved the promise that a rainbow brings. Through the storms, the darkness, and the trying times, the sign of the rainbow ensures that things are going to be ok. That through the unrest, there will be peace.

Even if you're not a religious person, the sentiment that the rainbow was a sign from God to every living creature that he would never flood the world again, is beautiful and another promise that we will all be ok in the end.

I created this image from one of the most peaceful locations I know of in New Zealand, one that is still very much unknown and off the tourist trail. I have spent many sunrises here, watching the alpen glow, smiling at the birds having their early morning swim, and taking in the incredible peaks that form the Southern Alps of New Zealand. 

On this day I arrived just after a snow storm had hit the area the day before, and the mountains had a fresh coat of frosting. As the sun rose it painted the peaks pastel pink and the water reflected them in perfect unison. To me this image is the symbolism of what the rainbow represents, the calm after the storm and a brighter future.

metadata.name: The Floating Maze
metadata.description:
Tucked away in southern India, is a hidden gem. Here movement creates magic, resulting in a kaleidoscope of leaves and the birth of a floating maze.
This is a freshwater lake that is home to numerous resident wetland birds as well as a few seasonal visitors. The lake is laden with floating water plants and lotus blooms. But there is more to it than that. The geometric design that you see in the image is actually a fluid pattern, ever-changing, constantly drifting. As traditional rowing boats seen in the centre of the image gently slice through the floating carpet of freshwater flora, they create breaks in the continuous sheet. When viewed from this vantage point, they occasionally make a perfect design that can be appreciated. This was one such day. The breeze was gentle, the boats felt poetic and this beautiful symmetry was created. 
Just as they converged in the epicentre, I knew at that moment, this was their masterpiece that deserved to be captured.
